week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 9-1 demolition in Week 1, balanced offense with four HR hitters, three solid SP performances — the most complete team in the league right now | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Trout (6 R, 3 HR, 5 RBI, .625 OBP) and Murakami both went deep three times in a week; offense is historically dangerous and 8-2 reflects it | same

3. General Hospital | Tied despite 23 RBI, .414 OBP, and 6 HR — this roster is elite and the tie feels more like bad luck than a weakness; adding Kumar Rocker deepens an already strong rotation | same
4. North Correa | Chris Sale was untouchable (15 K, 0.75 WHIP, 2 QS), Ohtani contributed on both sides, and a 7-3 win over the Reapers shows legitimate depth | up
5. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge and Will Smith went deep, Sanchez was filthy (0.50 WHIP), and a 5-4 gritty win over Hiroshima shows this team can grind out victories | same
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Seven HR, three QS, and a 2.98 ERA in a tie — the pitching is real, the offense is emerging, and Brendan Donovan's multi-position versatility is a huge roster asset | up
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Alvarez and Aranda mashed, 43 K from the staff, but a 6.00 ERA and zero QS in 39 IP cost them dearly; adding Merrill Kelly is the right move | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Lost 4-5 despite 44 K and a 2.35 ERA — Skubal and Bradley were sharp, but Eovaldi's 9.64 ERA and a completely frozen stolen base column killed them | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 0.33 WHIP and Yelich's .600 OBP were electric; four aggressive waiver adds signal a manager who knows this roster needs reinforcing | same
10. Benchwarmers | Lucky 7-3 win masks a thin roster, but Michael King (14 K, 1.64 ERA) and Spencer Arrighetti (8 K, 1.29 ERA) give them a real two-ace foundation | up
11. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease was sharp (1.69 ERA) but Aaron Nola got rocked (8.38 ERA); an 0-HR week in a power-scoring league is a brutal structural flaw | down
12. Riverside Roughnecks | Lost 1-9 in the most lopsided result of the week; Langeliers hit 3 HR but the pitching was historically bad (7.91 ERA, 0 QS, 19.1 IP) | down
13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 39 K is impressive but Matthew Boyd's 14.73 ERA and Tyler Wells' 10.80 ERA are disqualifying; only 1 HR all week in a power league is alarming | same
14. Buttville Skidmarks | DeLauter slugging 4 HR can't paper over a .271 OBP and 6.02 ERA — until the pitching stabilizes, this team will keep losing winnable matchups | same
week_02: 1. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Historic 9-0 shutout anchored by Ben Rice's monster week and Mason Miller's 0.30 WHIP; this team just announced it belongs at the top | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Most consistent team in the league across all four weeks, 8-1 category win this week, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both mashing with Chase Burns dealing — the floor here is elite | same
3. BaseballPro77 | Gutted out a 5-4 win on fumes offensively, but the roster depth and active management keep them near the top; the ERA concern is real and needs fixing fast | down
4. General Hospital | Six category wins with a 2.10 ERA and only 5 HR — this offense has legitimate power gaps but the pitching and on-base skills carry them; Kumar Rocker addition could be huge | up
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and a staff-wide 2.25 ERA powered a 6-4 win; tied the league K record and the rotation with Ashcraft, Luzardo, and Burke is genuinely deep | up
6. Riverside Roughnecks | Exploded for 38 runs and 10 HR in a 7-3 win — Otto Lopez, Xavier Edwards, Jackson Merrill, Kerry Carpenter all contributing; is this the breakout week they needed? | up
7. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Duran all scoring runs; the .267 OBP is alarming and 46 IP with 46 K is thin rotation usage — needs more from the arms | down
8. North Correa | Oneil Cruz went off for 4 HR and 10 RBI and they still only won 7-3; rotation outside Senga needs reinforcement and the Austin Wells addition at catcher is smart | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 26 K and 1.65 ERA was absolutely elite but Freddie Freeman's .226 OBP is deeply concerning; four waiver adds show urgency and energy from the manager | same

10. Hiroshima Radiation | Dropped 8-1 in a bloodbath despite Carlos Correa scoring 8 runs and Schwarber going deep 3 times; a 43 K staff week is the core problem and Colin Rea won't fix it alone | down
11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 6-4 but Altuve's .577 OBP, Walker, Henderson, and Díaz all went deep — the offense is real; Aaron Nola and Seth Lugo both getting torched is the recurring nightmare | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite a 3.19 ERA and 4 QS — the pitching did its job and still couldn't cover for the offense; Parker Messick and Gausman both brilliant and it didn't matter | same
13. Benchwarmers | Won 5-4 but this is a smoke-and-mirrors record; Corbin Carroll and Cody Bellinger are real pieces but the rotation logged only 46 IP and the power numbers are near-invisible | same

14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Got blanked 9-0 in every single category — zero wins, zero stolen bases, a .270 OBP, and Imanaga struggled again; the aggressive waiver moves including Masyn Winn and Dustin May signal panic mode | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8-2 win, league-best ERA (2.11), 9 SB, 5 QS, and Matt Olson going 3 HR/8 RBI — the most complete team in the league through three weeks | same
2. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker went 5 HR/6 RBI in a week — five home runs from one player — and Logan Gilbert anchored a rotation that posted 5 QS; still the deepest roster top to bottom | same

3. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (.636 OBP, 3 HR, 8 R, 8 RBI) and Willson Contreras (3 HR, 11 RBI, .594 OBP) combined for one of the best dual-star performances of the season; 6-3 win cements their top-tier status | up
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Best week of the season: 8 category wins, 6 QS, 75 K, .386 OBP, Bradley and McLean both elite — this rotation is for real and the offense finally showed up | up
5. General Hospital | Tied when their stats said they should have won by a wide margin; 70 K, 30 RBI, and Manny Machado (3 HR, 11 RBI) are legitimately scary, but a 5.48 ERA keeps costing them | same
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Lost 5-4 despite leading the league in IP and putting up 5 QS and 7 NSVH — the rotation is elite and this team is severely undervalued by their record | up
7. North Correa | Ohtani (4 HR, .529 OBP) and Chris Sale (22 K) are a devastating one-two punch but the rest of the roster is thin; 11 SB ties the season record and shows their identity | same
8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8-2 by the Skeeters but still put up 75 K and Yordan Alvarez hit 2 HR; adding Merrill Kelly is a smart, high-floor rotation move | down

9. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6-3 but their pitching ERA (1.65) and WHIP (1.04) were legitimately elite — Taylor Ward, Kris Bubic, and Brandon Woodruff all looked sharp; offense is the persistent weakness | same

10. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison (13 K, 1.74 ERA) and Landen Roupp (10 K, 2.63 ERA) gave them a real rotation backbone; four waiver adds show aggressive management trying to close roster holes | same
11. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-3 despite zero QS and a 5.04 ERA — Trevor Story's 9 RBI carried them; this team wins ugly and will eventually need pitching to hold | down
12. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz (2 HR, 4 SB) and a 2.25 ERA are the floor here, but a tie when they should have pushed for a win reflects persistent inconsistency | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson (5 HR, 8 R, 8 RBI) had a truly spectacular week and it still wasn't enough — this roster's depth problem is a structural issue, not a slump | same
14. Benchwarmers | Won on Michael King's back alone; bottom-three in HR, K, ERA, and WHIP — the luck delta of +0.91 is the highest in the league and a regression warning sign is flashing red | same
week_04: 1. BaseballPro77 | Record-setting 19 HR and 39 RBI with a 2.33 ERA — this is the most complete performance of the season and the hottest team in the league right now | up
2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Lost the matchup but still put up 72 K and 4 QS; Ryan Weathers (18 K, 1.05 WHIP) is an ace-level performer and this offense remains elite over four weeks | same

3. General Hospital | An 8-2 blowout win driven by 6 QS, a 2.68 ERA, a 0.96 WHIP, and Will Warren (17 K, 1.69 ERA) — this pitching staff is the best in the league and Kumar Rocker adds even more depth | up
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge (5 HR, 7 RBI), Bailey Ober (17 K), and a 3.01 ERA with 5 QS in a clean 5-2 win shows the rotation is finally healthy enough to complement the elite offense | up

5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Eight quality starts is a league record-threatening number; Paul Skenes (0.90 ERA, 0.50 WHIP), Imanaga, and Valdez make this the deepest rotation in the game, even if the offense remains quiet | up

6. Albuquerque Bombers | Byron Buxton (3 HR, 8 R) and Ivan Herrera (2 HR) fueled a 5-4 win; aggressive waiver activity keeps plugging holes, and a Week 5 matchup with the struggling Reapers is a golden opportunity | same

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez (4 HR, 5 SB, .533 OBP) and Yordan Alvarez (4 HR, 7 RBI) are as good a 1-2 punch as any in the league; the -0.61 luck delta is a real concern but the talent is undeniable | up

8. North Correa | Nico Hoerner (11 RBI) and a 5 QS week show real depth, but a 5.56 ERA and only 2 category wins in the matchup confirm this team needs pitching help badly despite the Ohtani and stolen-base upside | same

9. Hiroshima Radiation | Skubal and Eovaldi both posted sub-1.50 ERAs and the team ERA was a stellar 2.81, yet they lost 6-4 — the -1 NSVH is a killer and the offense's 6 HR and 1 SB simply aren't enough firepower | down

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A 4-4 tie feels like a missed opportunity against a weak Wombats squad, but Misiorowski (14 K) and a solid bullpen show real pitching upside; the offense needs more punch than Xavier Edwards walks can provide | same

11. Canberra Wombats | Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (5 R) gave the offense life in a tie, and 4 category wins is an improvement — but a .283 OBP and 49.2 IP are troublingly low roster-utilization numbers | up

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Junior Caminero (3 HR) and Bryan Reynolds (.500 OBP, 7 RBI) are legitimate weapons, but MacKenzie Gore's 6.52 ERA and Gausman's 1.55 WHIP continue to doom this team in the pitching categories | same

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Losing 7-2 to the hottest team in the league stings less than the underlying numbers — 33 hits, a .257 OBP, and only 2 category wins confirm this offense is broken, and dropping McGreevy without a real replacement is worrying | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 0.82 ERA was the lone bright spot in a 2-8 shellacking; with 4 HR, 45 K, and a +1.21 luck delta masking how bad things really are, this team is one average week away from a genuine crisis | down
week_05: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 52 runs and 15 HR in a single week is a statement; this offense is the most dangerous in the league and the Skeeters remain the most consistent team across all five weeks | up

2. General Hospital | A 5-5 tie stings when you expected a win, but Wrobleski's 0.69 ERA, Fried's 0.63 WHIP, and 5 QS confirm this rotation is elite; Kumar Rocker waiting in the wings makes this even scarier | same

3. BaseballPro77 | Lost 5-3 but Murakami (3 HR), Trout (2 HR, 2 SB), and Basallo (.611 OBP) all showed up — this offense is loaded and Strahm adds late-inning reliability to a staff that needed it | same

4. Athletic Park Dragons | Led the league with 13 HR and 39 RBI and still lost 6-4 because the pitching staff posted a 6.31 ERA with zero quality starts — the talent ceiling is enormous but the rotation is a genuine crisis | down

5. Albuquerque Bombers | An 8-1 demolition fueled by Harrison, Roupp, and a fully activated lineup; four waiver adds including Josh Jung show a manager aggressively patching holes, and this week proved the roster has real teeth | up

6. North Correa | Lost a heartbreaker 5-4 despite Glasnow posting a 0.25 WHIP and Sale a 0.50 WHIP — you simply cannot pitch better than that and lose; Ohtani's 3 SB and the 10-steal week show elite multi-category upside | same

7. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean's 17 K and 12 NSVH as a team are real weapons; the 5-5 tie feels like a loss given the opportunity, but the saves-and-holds structure makes this team uniquely dangerous in a close race | up

8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | A 5-3 win with Aranda (3 HR), Chisholm, and Griffin delivering is encouraging; the -0.43 luck delta continues to haunt but this roster is too talented to stay in the middle tier long | same

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-4 over a loaded Dragons squad — Kurtz (8 R, 3 HR), Suzuki (3 HR, .524 OBP), and 5 QS show a team that is quietly building something real; Ragans and Misiorowski both looked sharp | up

10. Canberra Wombats | Cal Raleigh (4 HR, 6 R) and Christian Walker (3 HR) gave the offense a pulse, but Aaron Nola's 11.00 ERA is a disaster and a team ERA of 5.59 with just 2 QS won't sustain ties, let alone wins | same

11. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The worst luck delta in the league (-0.62) is becoming an identity crisis; Harris (9 RBI), Moniak (2 HR), and solid pitching all week and they still can't get past a tie — Dustin May adds rotation hope | down

12. Buttville Skidmarks | De La Cruz and Caminero each went 3 HR and the team still only scored 24 runs; 5 NSVH is a real asset but Bradish's 2.03 WHIP is alarming and the offense is too inconsistent to trust | same

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got blasted 8-1 in every pitching category and the rotation gave up a 5.81 ERA; dropping McGreevy without a meaningful replacement signals a front office that doesn't have answers yet | down

14. Benchwarmers | The +1.33 luck delta is the highest in the league and the most unsustainable number in the data — 3 HR, a 5.28 ERA, and a thin power profile mean the house of cards is one bad week from collapsing | same
